text.title: Library Evaluation Rubric Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every Library Evaluation Rubric application must enforce. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Six axes scored 1-5: maintenance, license, bundle size, security history, ecosystem fit, learning curve. No axis is veto by itself; weighted sum decides.
rule.rationale: Single-axis evaluation produces bias; six-axis weighted decision is the empirically robust approach.

rule.statement: Check bus factor: contributors in last 12 months, sponsor org if any. <2 = high risk; ≥3 = acceptable; ≥10 = low risk.
rule.rationale: Bus factor predicts maintenance survivability; low bus factor = library will likely be abandoned within 18 months.

rule.statement: License is checked against project's distribution model. Copyleft (GPL/AGPL) is fine for internal tools, problematic for SaaS distribution.
rule.rationale: License errors at distribution time cost weeks; check at evaluation, not deploy.

rule.statement: If web/mobile, bundle impact measured. Library adding >5% of total bundle requires explicit approval.
rule.rationale: Bundle bloat compounds across many small choices; explicit threshold gates additions.

rule.statement: Check security history (last 3 years): unresolved high-severity CVEs are veto; resolved CVEs with reasonable timelines are OK.
rule.rationale: Resolved CVEs show responsiveness; unresolved = warning sign for future.
